other_observers: Mark Peterson; Mark found and identified this bird
specific_location: just south of Las Animas Fish Hatchery in field on ne corner of JJ Rd and Hwy 5.5 (Winding Rd) west of Las Animas and north of Hwy 194
description: A Plegadis ibis with 47 other ibis, the bird stood out by having quite thick blue borders above and below the bare facial skin, which was quite dark blue. These borders did not extend behind the eye and did not meet in front of the eye. The bill was an odd brownish color. The legs were nearly entirely dark, with just a bit of pinkish-red at the tibio-tarsal joint.  The plumage was all dark, being a deep chestnut on the body with the darkest such being on the head. The wings were entirely iridescent, variably metallic green and purple, including the tertials, which did not contrast with the rest of the wing, particularly the greater coverts.Behaviors: foraging in flood-irrigated alfalfa fieldCall: none heardPlumage: Definitive Basic
